title: GetResponse vs Campaign Monitor (2025): Which Is Better?
meta_description: Compare GetResponse and Campaign Monitor. See pricing, features, and find the best email marketing tool for your needs.
h1: GetResponse vs Campaign Monitor: Detailed Comparison
intro: GetResponse and Campaign Monitor are both established email marketing platforms, but they serve different business needs. GetResponse positions itself as a comprehensive marketing suite with webinars and courses, while Campaign Monitor focuses on beautifully designed emails and multi-brand management. This comparison will help you determine which platform aligns best with your marketing goals and team structure.

comparison_table.tool_a: Online course creators and webinar hosts
comparison_table.tool_b: Agencies and teams managing multiple brands

comparison_table.tool_a: Email marketing, webinars, landing pages, automation, courses, funnels
comparison_table.tool_b: Template builder, journey designer, smart segments, multi-brand, analytics, transactional email

choose_a_if: You want a free tier to test the platform with up to 500 subscribers
You're an online course creator or webinar host needing integrated course and funnel tools
You need an all-in-one marketing platform beyond just email
You prefer a higher Trustpilot rating and customer satisfaction score
choose_b_if: You need to manage multiple brands or client accounts from one dashboard
You prioritize beautiful email template design and visual builders
You want the lowest starting price without commitment to a freemium model
You need transactional email capabilities as a core feature

faq.q: Is GetResponse better than Campaign Monitor?
faq.a: It depends on your needs. GetResponse offers more comprehensive features including webinars, courses, and landing pages, making it ideal for content creators. Campaign Monitor excels in multi-brand management and template design, making it better for agencies. GetResponse also has a stronger customer satisfaction rating overall.

faq.q: Which is cheaper, GetResponse or Campaign Monitor?
faq.a: GetResponse is cheaper if you're starting out, thanks to its free tier supporting 500 subscribers at no cost. Once you upgrade, Campaign Monitor starts at $11/month versus GetResponse's $19/month, but GetResponse offers significantly more features at that price point.

faq.q: Can I switch from Campaign Monitor to GetResponse?
faq.a: Yes, both platforms typically support CSV or standard email list imports. Campaign Monitor allows you to export your subscriber lists and email content, which can then be imported into GetResponse. The migration process is straightforward, though you may need to recreate automation workflows manually.
